title: School Based Integrated Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > School Based Integrated Services 12 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Hispanic/Latino Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LINN BENTON LINCOLN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT STUDENT AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves children with behavior, social service, and academic achievement needs. Also assists school districts by supporting them in the search for practical solutions to student behavior, instructional improvement, and social service needs in the following areas: -Attendance Services: Interventions and resources for staff and parents regarding school attendance and truancy. -Behavior Consultant Services: Support for students, parents, school staff, and community agencies to assist students with behavioral or emotional issues. Includes assessment, observation, interviews, safety risk assessment, and recommended interventions, such as counseling, skill training, and referrals to other services. -Family Support Project: Provides information and referrals to community resources, youth activities, home-based parenting support, counseling, and more. -Youth Service Team (YST): Multidisciplinary teams provide integrated services to students in Linn County, including referrals to counseling and community-based services, youth and family activities, help navigating social service programs, outreach and crisis intervention, and more. 905 4th Avenue SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Varies by program Email (541) 812-2690 Get Directions Visit Website More Details METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading clubs, gender groups, conflict resolution classes, case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 1700 NW Civic Drive Suite 200 Gresham, OR 97080 Eligibility: Grades 5-12 who are at risk of academic failure and their families Hours: Varies Email (971) 506-2609 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIVERVIEW CENTER FOR GROWTH INTENSIVE SCHOOL AND COMMUNITY BASED PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Intensive School Based Program provides a specialized and individualized set of in-home and community based mental health services in the child's home and at public school. Integrated treatment and education program geared towards elementary aged children who have severe mental, emotional and behavioral disorders. Intensive Community Based Treatment Services (ICTS) Outpatient Unit provides psychiatric care management to children and their families. 3995 Marcola Road Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Intensive School Based Program: Children ages 6-12; call for detailed eligibility criteria Intensive Community Based Treatment Services: Children ages 3-18; call for detailed eligibility criteria Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 726-1465 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SELF ENHANCEMENT INC YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers in-school case management and support as well as after-school enrochment programs. Services include mentoring, case management, parenting classes, and referrals to outside resources. Parent coordinators work with family service staff to help parents find housing, employment, and counseling in order to provide a safe environment for their children. 3920 N Kerby Avenue Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Grades 2nd-12th and attendence at partnering school. Check website for list of partnering schools. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-7pm, Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 249-1721 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH LANE SCHOOL DISTRICT THE EARLY LEARNING CENTER COMMUNITY CARE SPECIALIST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a family access program that coordinates with district supports and provides parent outreach, connections to community resources, coordination of family services, and alignment with school-level family outreach events and goals. 1310 S 8th Street Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (541) 767-3529 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TODOS JUNTOS BAKER PRAIRIE MIDDLE SCHOOL FAMILY RESOURCE NAVIGATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists families in navigating local, school, county, and state resources. Works to identify services that meet the immediate needs of the referred family and find the supports that match those needs. Services include family advocacy, interpretation services, information and referral, clothing and school supplies, transportation, connection to health and food resources, assistance with developmental screenings, diaper assistance, housing and shelter assistance, parent education and family support, and employment specialists. 1859 S Township Road Canby, OR 97013 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 Hours: By appointment Email (503) 208-6201 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TODOS JUNTOS CECILE TROST ELEMENTARY SCHOOL FAMILY RESOURCE NAVIGATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists families in navigating local, school, county, and state resources.
